comment: Separable orthogonal coordinate systems. The 11 systems in 3D in which the Helmholtz/Laplace equation is R-separable are listed in Morse & Feshbach (1953), Methods of Theoretical Physics, Vol. I, ch. 5. The 2D analogues (confocal coordinate families) are also separable for the 2D Helmholtz equation. Renderer is schematic вЂ” only one or two iso-surface slices are drawn.

primitives.name: Cartesian (2D)
primitives.notes: Trivial separability. Two orthogonal families of straight lines.

primitives.name: polar (2D)
primitives.notes: Concentric circles + radial lines. Helmholtz separates into Bessel times exp(inП†).

primitives.name: parabolic (2D)
primitives.notes: Two orthogonal families of confocal parabolas with common focus at origin and axis along x. Used in the Stark-effect parabolic separation of hydrogen.

primitives.name: elliptic (2D)
primitives.notes: Confocal ellipses + hyperbolae sharing foci at В±a. Helmholtz separates into Mathieu functions.

primitives.name: Cartesian (3D)
primitives.notes: First of the eleven 3D separable systems.

primitives.name: circular cylindrical (3D)
primitives.notes: Polar in the xy-plane, extruded along z. Bessel-times-exponential basis.

primitives.name: elliptic cylindrical (3D)

basisFunctions: Mathieu functions Г— e^{i k_z z}

primitives.notes: 2D elliptic in the xy-plane extruded along z. Solves for elliptic waveguide modes.

primitives.name: parabolic cylindrical (3D)

basisFunctions: parabolic cylinder D_ОЅ Г— e^{i k_z z}

primitives.notes: 2D parabolic in the xy-plane extruded along z. Diffraction by a half-plane uses these.

primitives.name: spherical (3D)

attrs.metric: ds^2 = dr^2 + r^2 dОё^2 + r^2 sin^2Оё dП†^2

primitives.notes: Default for any centrally-symmetric problem. Hydrogen atom separates here.

primitives.name: prolate spheroidal (3D)
primitives.notes: Confocal prolate ellipsoids + two-sheet hyperboloids of revolution. Used for the H2+ molecule (two-center problem).

primitives.name: oblate spheroidal (3D)
primitives.notes: Confocal oblate ellipsoids + one-sheet hyperboloids. Earth-shape geodesy and electromagnetic disk problems.

primitives.name: parabolic (3D)
primitives.notes: Confocal paraboloids of revolution. Hydrogen Stark effect separates here, not in spherical.

primitives.name: conical (3D)

attrs.metric: ds^2 = dr^2 + r^2(dОё^2 + dО»^2 with elliptic angular metric)

primitives.notes: Spherical r times two confocal-cone angular variables. LamГ© functions arise.

primitives.name: ellipsoidal (3D)

attrs.metric: ds^2 = sum (...)/(P(О»,Ој,ОЅ)) terms; see Morse-Feshbach В§5.1

primitives.notes: Three families of confocal quadrics: ellipsoid, one-sheet hyperboloid, two-sheet hyperboloid sharing the same foci. Most general orthogonal separable system in 3D.

primitives.name: paraboloidal (3D)

attrs.metric: ds^2 in terms of three confocal paraboloids

primitives.notes: Three families of confocal paraboloids. The three-dimensional paraboloidal limit of ellipsoidal coordinates.
